body.title: feat: self-directed rollback — Igor reverts his own bad changes
body.result: cancelled — Igor coding retired (D-worker-daemon-retired-2026-05-25); self-directed rollback shelved
body.description:
## Imported from GH#210 (created 2026-03-13, open)

When Igor makes a self-edit that regresses behavior, he should be able to roll it back himself — without human intervention. The arbiter only gets involved if he can't resolve it.

After a self-edit + test cycle, if tests fail or behavior regresses, Igor calls `rollback_last_edit()` which reverts the last `patch_source_file` operation (git revert or file-level undo). All rollbacks logged to forensic_logger self_edit channel. Arbiter notification if rollback fails or for entangled multi-edit rollback.

Tools sketched: `rollback_last_edit(reason)`, `list_recent_self_edits(n)`, `rollback_to(commit_sha, reason)`.

Why this matters: without rollback, self-editing is dangerous at speed. With rollback, Igor can iterate aggressively — patch, test, revert if needed — knowing he can't permanently break himself. Part of epic #206.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/self_edit.py (existing — extend with rollback functions); wild_igor/igor/tools/forensic_logger.py (self_edit channel logging); tests/test_self_edit_rollback.py.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not; safety-first (rollback path must be more reliable than the patch path).

**Scope boundary:** IN — rollback_last_edit + list_recent_self_edits + rollback_to + forensic logging + arbiter escalation on rollback failure. OUT — automatic rollback triggers (Igor decides to call rollback explicitly); rollback across instances; rollback of non-self-edit commits.

**Test plan:** tests/test_self_edit_rollback.py — patch a file, call rollback, assert file reverted; test entangled multi-edit rollback via rollback_to; test arbiter escalation when rollback itself fails. Smoke test: full patch+test+revert cycle on a throwaway test file.

**Status:** Pending IGOR_SELF_EDIT_ENABLED unfreezing — useful but not actionable until self-edit is broadly enabled.
